docs: https://api.configcat.com/docs/ (rendered from https://api.configcat.com/docs/v1/swagger.json, the published OpenAPI 3.0 spec)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against ConfigCat's published OpenAPI spec (107 documented method+path combinations). This bundle covers every practical GET list/detail read (25 streams, up from 5) and every dialect-expressible non-destructive-admin write action (12 write actions across configs/environments/settings(flags)/tags: create+update+delete for each). Excluded: flag VALUE mutation (the get/replace/update-value family across v1/v2 settings/environments paths) -- ConfigCat's own docs distinguish flag METADATA (name/hint/tags/order, modeled here) from flag VALUE (the per-environment targeting rules/rollout percentages actually served to SDKs, a JSON-Patch-shaped body this pass does not model to avoid silently corrupting live flag evaluation logic for running applications); organization/product/environment-admin membership and integration-configuration mutations (invite/remove members, Jira/webhook/integration-link wiring, proxy-profile SDK-key selection); and code-reference/upload endpoints (a CI/CD-tool integration surface, not CLM-shaped record data). Every excluded endpoint below carries the specific reason it was not modeled.

excluded.reason: single-product detail already enumerable via the covered products stream's own fan-out-free list; not modeled as a separate detail stream.
renames/reconfigures a product; deferred pending its own schema review, distinct from the config/environment-level create/update actions this pass models.

permanently deletes an entire ConfigCat product and every config/environment/flag within it; destructive beyond this pass's write scope (configs/environments/flags/tags only, not the product container itself).
creates a brand-new product within an organization (tenant-structure provisioning), an organization-admin action outside this pass's write scope.
a partial-update variant of the already-covered update_flag (PUT) action; not modeled separately this pass.
CI/CD code-scanning integration data (which source files reference this flag), a distinct developer-tooling surface unrelated to this connector's core CLM/flag-metadata streams.
predefined value/variation templates for a setting; deferred pending its own schema review, distinct from the setting metadata already covered.
updates predefined variations; deferred alongside the read side.

flag VALUE (per-environment targeting rules/rollout percentages actually served to SDKs) is a distinct, JSON-Patch/complex-targeting-rule shape from flag METADATA (name/hint/tags/order) this pass models; not modeled to avoid silently corrupting live flag evaluation logic for running applications.
replaces a flag's evaluated value/targeting rules; deferred alongside the read side of flag-value data, for the same live-evaluation-safety reason.
JSON-Patch-shaped partial update to a flag's value/targeting rules; deferred alongside the read side, for the same live-evaluation-safety reason.
v2 variant of the already-excluded flag-value read; same live-evaluation-safety reason as the v1 path.
v2 variant of the already-excluded flag-value write; same live-evaluation-safety reason as the v1 path.
v2 variant of the already-excluded flag-value write; same live-evaluation-safety reason as the v1 path.
an environment+setting-scoped alternate path for the same flag-value data excluded above; same live-evaluation-safety reason.
environment+setting-scoped flag-value write; same live-evaluation-safety reason as the settingKeyOrId path.
environment+setting-scoped flag-value write; same live-evaluation-safety reason as the settingKeyOrId path.
v2 variant of the same excluded environment+setting-scoped flag-value data.
v2 variant of the same excluded environment+setting-scoped flag-value write.
v2 variant of the same excluded environment+setting-scoped flag-value write.
bulk-posts flag VALUE changes across every setting in a config+environment at once; same live-evaluation-safety reason as the single-flag-value exclusions above.
v2 variant of the already-covered config_setting_values read; not modeled as a separate stream (the v1 path is covered).
v2 variant of the bulk flag-value write already excluded at v1, same live-evaluation-safety reason.

creates a webhook scoped to a specific config+environment (a variant creation path distinct from the covered webhooks/webhook read streams); write-side webhook creation deferred pending its own schema review.
replaces an existing webhook's URL/headers/trigger config; deferred alongside webhook creation.
partially updates a webhook; deferred alongside webhook creation.
deletes a webhook; deferred alongside webhook creation.
webhook HMAC signing key material; a credential-shaped value, not connector-relevant record data.
third-party integration-link (e.g. Jira ticket) management on a specific flag; a narrow cross-tool wiring feature outside this pass's write scope.
creates a third-party integration link on a specific flag; deferred alongside the delete side.
resolves integration-link detail metadata for a third-party key; a narrow cross-tool wiring feature, not core CLM/flag data.
establishes an org-level Jira OAuth connection; an organization-admin integration-provisioning action outside this pass's write scope.
creates a Jira-specific integration link on a flag; deferred alongside the generic integration-link exclusions above.
deletes a configured third-party integration connection; organization/product-admin configuration surface outside this pass's write scope.
reconfigures a third-party integration connection; organization/product-admin configuration surface outside this pass's write scope.
creates a new third-party integration connection; organization/product-admin configuration surface outside this pass's write scope.
revokes a pending user invitation; an organization-admin user-provisioning action outside this pass's write scope.
pending organization-level invitations list; deferred pending its own schema review, a distinct user-provisioning surface from this connector's core CLM streams.
pending product-level invitations list; same narrowing as the organization-level invitations list.
removes a member from the organization; an organization-admin user-management action outside this pass's write scope.
updates an organization member's permissions; an organization-admin user-management action outside this pass's write scope.
plan/usage-limitation metadata for the organization; deferred, a narrow supplementary read not yet reviewed for schema shape.
product-scoped member listing, a narrower variant of the already-covered organization-level members stream (every product member is also an organization member).
invites a new user into the product; a product-admin user-provisioning action outside this pass's write scope.
removes a member from the product; a product-admin user-management action outside this pass's write scope.
product-level preference settings (e.g. default reason-required/mandatory-tagging policy); deferred, a narrow supplementary read not yet reviewed for schema shape.
updates product-level preference settings; a product-admin configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
deletes a custom permission group; a product-admin access-control configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
updates a custom permission group's capability grants; a product-admin access-control configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
creates a custom permission group; a product-admin access-control configuration action outside this pass's write scope.

updates a segment's comparison-rule targeting definition; deferred pending its own schema review -- a segment mutation, unlike this pass's flag-metadata mutations, changes live targeting behavior for every flag that references it.
deletes a segment; deferred alongside the update side, same live-targeting-behavior concern.
creates a segment; deferred alongside update/delete for the same live-targeting-behavior concern, kept consistent (all three modeled together or none this pass).
lists flags carrying a given tag, derivable by filtering the already-covered settings stream's own tags array; not modeled as a separate stream.
deletes a ConfigCat Proxy deployment profile; an infrastructure-configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
replaces a ConfigCat Proxy deployment profile; an infrastructure-configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
partially updates a ConfigCat Proxy deployment profile; an infrastructure-configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
creates a ConfigCat Proxy deployment profile; an infrastructure-configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
SDK keys selected for a Proxy profile; a credential-adjacent listing tied to the excluded Proxy-profile write surface, not modeled this pass.
modifies which SDK keys a Proxy profile serves; an infrastructure-configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
modifies which SDK keys a Proxy profile serves; an infrastructure-configuration action outside this pass's write scope.
generates a new Proxy profile secret credential; a credential-generation action, never modeled as a connector write.
uploads CI/CD code-scanning references (which source files reference which flags); a developer-tooling ingestion surface, not CLM record data.
deletes uploaded code-reference reports; deferred alongside code-reference upload.
organization-scoped audit-log listing, a broader-scoped variant of the same audit-log data the already-covered product-scoped audit_logs stream exposes; not modeled as a separate stream.
v2 variant of the same audit-log data; the v1 product-scoped audit_logs stream already covers this connector's audit-log surface.
v2 variant of the already-covered members stream; not modeled as a separate stream.
v2 variant of the already-covered audit_logs stream; not modeled as a separate stream.
